op.name: where
op.notes: Select elements based on condition. Cost: numel(input).
op.summary: Return elements chosen from `x` or `y` depending on `condition`.

blocks.text: Return elements chosen from `x` or `y` depending on `condition`.

blocks.name: note
blocks.body:
When only `condition` is provided, this function is a shorthand for
    ``np.asarray(condition).nonzero()``. Using `nonzero` directly should be
    preferred, as it behaves correctly for subclasses. The rest of this
    documentation covers only the case where all three arguments are
    provided.
blocks.raw:
.. note::
    When only `condition` is provided, this function is a shorthand for
    ``np.asarray(condition).nonzero()``. Using `nonzero` directly should be
    preferred, as it behaves correctly for subclasses. The rest of this
    documentation covers only the case where all three arguments are
    provided.

desc_blocks.text:
Values from which to choose. `x`, `y` and `condition` need to be
broadcastable to some shape.

An array with elements from `x` where `condition` is True, and elements
from `y` elsewhere.

blocks.text: If all the arrays are 1-D, `where` is equivalent to::

[xv if c else yv
     for c, xv, yv in zip(condition, x, y)]
This can be used on multidimensional arrays too:
The shapes of x, y, and the condition are broadcast together:

blocks.code:
>>> x, y = np.ogrid[:3, :4]
>>> np.where(x < y, x, 10 + y)  # both x and 10+y are broadcast
array([[10,  0,  0,  0],
       [10, 11,  1,  1],
       [10, 11, 12,  2]])

blocks.text: nonzero — The function that is called when x and y are omitted
